[The Sulikas are assigned to the north-west and south-east
divisions (XIV) 23,8). They are also called Culikas in the Puranas.
The Matsya-purana refers to their country as being watered by the
river Caksu (Oxus). Scholars identify them with the Sogdians living
to the north of the Oxus, with the Calukyas of the Deccan etc.
They were also identified with the Sulikas of Orissa. GUndhara
extended from the Kabul valley to Taksasila and comprised the
Rawalpindi and Peshawar districts. The town Puskalavat! is men-
-tioned by our author.
The tavanas were the Greek settlers found in the south-west
divison near Nasik.
The Videha country comprised the country from Gorakhpur
on the Rapti to Darbhanga, with Kosala on the west and Ahga on
the east.
The §akas are mentiond in many inscriptions beginning with
that of Rudradaman. They are assigned to the western division.
